note: User provided comprehensive UX vision covering 20 features for desktop agent OS. Top 5 priorities: (1) Operating Picture — live mission dashboard, (2) Agent Diff Review — explainable/revertible changes, (3) Safe Stop / Resume / Checkpoint Timeline, (4) Session/Event Inspector (in flight), (5) Command Palette — keyboard-first IDE surface. This UX layer maps cleanly on top of the Runtime Event system and DuckDB analytics already in flight. Should decompose into 5 new lanes after current 4 complete. The "Why did you do that?" and "Touch set preview" features are particularly high-value for trust.

Session built complete Runtime Event infrastructure across 4 concurrent lanes. Key observations:

1. Lane-owner parallel fanout works well — all 4 completed independently.
2. Cross-lane file coordination (event/index.ts shared between Lane 1 and Lane 3) needs explicit handoff — Lane 3's index.ts correctly re-exports Lane 1's exports but this required Lane 3 to wait for Lane 1.
3. smart_find doesn't recurse into subdirectories by default — had to use max_depth parameter to discover debug/ files.
4. Flight recorder location was at src/debug/ not src/event/debug/ — both are valid but should be consistent.
5. The session-viz context (in-memory) and inspector context (also in-memory with 10K cap) both subscribe to the same SDK event stream — potential for duplicate connections. Should either share one connection or ensure the SDK handles multiple subscribers.
6. The event-bridge.ts Bus subscriber layer needs to be wired into the main app startup — currently created but not integrated into the Layer composition. This is the main integration gap.
